assertion_results.text: Tasks target both 'trustify-backend' and 'trustify-ui' repositories with at least one task per repo
assertion_results.evidence: Backend tasks: Task 1 (Repository: trustify-backend), Task 2 (trustify-backend), Task 3 (trustify-backend), Task 4 (trustify-backend), Task 9 (trustify-backend). Frontend tasks: Task 5 (Repository: trustify-ui), Task 6 (trustify-ui), Task 7 (trustify-ui), Task 8 (trustify-ui). Both repos have multiple tasks.

assertion_results.text: Backend tasks have lower task numbers than the frontend tasks that depend on them
assertion_results.evidence: Backend tasks are Tasks 1-4 (trustify-backend). Frontend tasks are Tasks 5-8 (trustify-ui). Task 5 (frontend) depends on Task 1, Task 6 depends on Task 5, Task 7 depends on Task 6, Task 8 depends on Task 7. The dependency chain crosses repos: frontend Task 5 depends on backend Task 1. Backend tasks 2-4 form their own chain. All backend implementation tasks (2,3,4) have lower numbers than all frontend implementation tasks (5,6,7,8).

assertion_results.text: UI-facing frontend tasks (pages, components) reference Figma design context mentioning specific PatternFly components and visual specifications — API-layer frontend tasks (API types, client functions, hooks) are exempt from this requirement
assertion_results.evidence: Task 6 (SbomComparePage - UI-facing) references Figma design in Description ('following the Figma design') and mentions specific PatternFly components: Select (with typeahead), Button (primary), Dropdown, ExpandableSection, Badge (with color specs: green/red/blue/yellow), Table (composable, sortable), EmptyState (with CodeBranchIcon), Skeleton. Task 7 (SbomListPage modification - UI-facing) mentions PatternFly table row selection and PatternFly secondary Button. Task 5 (API types, client function, hook - API-layer) does not reference Figma and is exempt. Task 8 (E2E tests) does not reference Figma but is a test task, not a UI-facing component task.

assertion_results.text: Cross-repo dependency ordering is enforced: either at least one frontend task explicitly depends on a backend task in its Dependencies section, or all tasks target a shared feature branch where backend tasks have lower task numbers than frontend tasks (implicit ordering via feature-branch workflow)
assertion_results.evidence: All intermediate tasks target branch TC-9003 (feature-branch workflow). Backend tasks 2,3,4 have lower task numbers than frontend tasks 5,6,7,8. Additionally, the merge task (Task 9) depends on all implementation tasks from both repos. While no individual frontend task explicitly depends on a backend task (Task 5 depends on Task 1 which is a bookend, not an implementation task), the feature-branch workflow with lower backend task numbers satisfies the implicit ordering criterion.

assertion_results.text: Frontend tasks reference paths from repo-frontend.md; backend tasks reference paths from repo-backend.md
assertion_results.evidence: Backend tasks reference paths from repo-backend.md: Task 2 references modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/ (exists in manifest), Task 3 references modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/ (exists), Task 4 references modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/ (exists). Frontend tasks reference paths from repo-frontend.md: Task 5 references src/api/models.ts, src/api/rest.ts, src/hooks/ (all exist in manifest), Task 6 references src/pages/, src/routes.tsx, src/components/SeverityBadge.tsx (all in manifest), Task 7 references src/pages/SbomListPage/SbomListPage.tsx (in manifest), Task 8 references tests/e2e/, tests/mocks/handlers.ts (in manifest).

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Repository section with a single repository name (not multiple repos per task)
assertion_results.evidence: All 9 tasks have a '## Repository' section with a single repo name: Task 1: trustify-backend, Task 2: trustify-backend, Task 3: trustify-backend, Task 4: trustify-backend, Task 5: trustify-ui, Task 6: trustify-ui, Task 7: trustify-ui, Task 8: trustify-ui, Task 9: trustify-backend. No task lists multiple repositories.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements sections as required by the handoff contract in task-description-template.md
assertion_results.evidence: All 9 tasks contain: '## Target Branch' (Task 1: main, Tasks 2-8: TC-9003, Task 9: main), '## Description' (present in all), '## Acceptance Criteria' (present in all with checkbox items), '## Test Requirements' (present in all with checkbox items).

assertion_results.text: File paths in Files to Modify and Files to Create reference paths from the corresponding mock repository structure manifests (repo-backend.md or repo-frontend.md), not invented paths
assertion_results.evidence: Backend 'Files to Modify' paths reference existing manifest paths: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/mod.rs (in manifest), modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/mod.rs (in manifest), modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs (in manifest), server/src/main.rs (in manifest). 'Files to Create' paths extend existing directories from the manifest: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/comparison.rs (new file in existing model/ dir), modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/compare.rs (new file in existing service/ dir), modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/compare.rs (new file in existing endpoints/ dir). Frontend 'Files to Modify' paths: src/api/models.ts (in manifest), src/api/rest.ts (in manifest), src/routes.tsx (in manifest), src/pages/SbomListPage/SbomListPage.tsx (in manifest), tests/mocks/handlers.ts (in manifest). Frontend 'Files to Create' paths extend existing directories: src/hooks/useSbomComparison.ts (new file in existing hooks/ dir), src/pages/SbomComparePage/ (new page directory following existing page pattern), tests/e2e/sbom-compare.spec.ts (new file in existing e2e/ dir).

assertion_results.text: Implementation Notes in every non-bookend task reference specific file paths and code patterns from the repository, not abstract or generic guidance — bookend tasks (create-branch, merge-branch) are exempt as they omit Implementation Notes by design
assertion_results.evidence: Task 1 (bookend create-branch): no Implementation Notes - exempt. Task 2: references modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/summary.rs, modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/details.rs, modules/fundamental/src/package/model/summary.rs, modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/summary.rs with specific patterns (serde derives, field patterns). Task 3: references modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs (SbomService), modules/fundamental/src/package/service/mod.rs (PackageService), modules/fundamental/src/advisory/service/advisory.rs (AdvisoryService), common/src/error.rs with specific patterns. Task 4: references modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/get.rs, list.rs, mod.rs with specific Axum patterns. Task 5: references src/api/models.ts, src/api/rest.ts, src/api/client.ts, src/hooks/useSboms.ts, src/hooks/useSbomById.ts with specific code patterns. Task 6: references src/pages/SbomDetailPage/, src/hooks/useSboms.ts, src/hooks/useSbomComparison.ts, src/components/SeverityBadge.tsx with specific PatternFly component patterns. Task 7: references src/pages/SbomListPage/SbomListPage.tsx with specific PatternFly table patterns. Task 8: references tests/e2e/sbom-list.spec.ts with specific Playwright patterns. Task 9 (bookend merge-branch): no Implementation Notes - exempt.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Target Branch section — either all set to 'main' (direct-to-main workflow) or bookend tasks set to 'main' with intermediate tasks set to the feature issue ID (feature-branch workflow)
assertion_results.evidence: Feature-branch workflow detected. Bookend tasks: Task 1 (create-branch) Target Branch: main, Task 9 (merge-branch) Target Branch: main. Intermediate tasks: Task 2 Target Branch: TC-9003, Task 3: TC-9003, Task 4: TC-9003, Task 5: TC-9003, Task 6: TC-9003, Task 7: TC-9003, Task 8: TC-9003. This follows the feature-branch pattern correctly.

assertion_results.text: After each task is created, a description digest comment is posted with a real computed SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters, not a placeholder (<hex-digest>, <hex>), abbreviated value, or example string. Marker format: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256:<64-char-hex>'
assertion_results.evidence: No digest comments found in any output file. Searched all files for 'digest', 'sha256', 'sha-256' (case-insensitive) and found zero matches. The output files contain only task descriptions without any '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256:' markers.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: No convention applicability rationales found in any task. Searched all output files for 'Applies:' and 'Per CONVENTIONS' and found zero matches. The tasks reference file patterns and conventions informally (e.g., Task 2 mentions 'field conventions' in Reuse Candidates, Task 5 mentions 'query key conventions') but none include the prescribed format 'Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'. Convention-aware enrichment was not performed.
